data.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G4 (child consistency) of parent epic `5fd2` × child `012e` (Layer-1 DET floor). Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster. NET: no contradiction, no scope-overlap collision, no ordering/dependency gap that breaks the shipped slice. Two LOW residual-reference / implicit-shared-state consistency findings (both wording/seam, not contradictions). Modes that are CLEAN are affirmed at the end.

═══ FINDING 1 (mode: IMPLICIT SHARED STATE + CONSUMER IMPACT) — the DET-coverage payload (`det_coverage()`) is an implicitly-shared data structure flowing 012e → ca03 → 4bb7 with NO pinned cross-child contract ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child `012e` "## Scope"/AC#2 ("Coverage recorded on the attestation"; impl `det_floor.det_coverage()` emitting per-check ran/abstain/status); sibling `ca03` (stores it into `coverage["det"]`, orchestrator.py); sibling `4bb7` (`attest.build_manifest` binds the coverage counts into the HMAC-signed manifest).
- evidence:
  - 012e MINTS a coverage structure (per-check ran/abstain/status), ca03 ASSEMBLES it into the verdict `coverage` dict, and 4bb7 SIGNS coverage into the manifest. Three children share one undocumented data shape across the seam; no child declares the schema as a contract the others depend on (012e `deps: []`; ca03/4bb7 reference it only in prose + implementation).
  - The G3 container review on this same pairing already independently flagged the seam-drift risk (Finding 1 there): a future edit adding a `det_coverage()` key that ca03/4bb7 don't propagate would silently drop part of the DET coverage from the SIGNED attestation while every child still passes its own AC. That is precisely an implicit-shared-state consistency hazard for G4.
  - Roster cross-check: this is NOT an uncovered-criterion absence — both halves ship (012e emits, 4bb7 signs, verify-PASS on both). It is a CONSISTENCY note: the shared payload has no contract test spanning the three children, so the coupling is implicit.
- scenarios: `det_coverage()` gains/renames a per-check field; ca03 forwards only the old keys; 4bb7 signs the truncated set → the attestation's "coverage recorded" (parent SC#2) silently omits part of the DET floor, undetected by any single child's tests.
- impact: Low — all three halves are implemented and individually verify-PASS; the risk is future drift across an implicit shared structure, not a present contradiction.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the DET-coverage payload as an explicit cross-child contract: add a shared test (or schema) asserting the keys 012e's `det_coverage()` emits survive ca03's assembly into `coverage["det"]` AND land in the field 4bb7 binds into the signed manifest — so the implicit 012e→ca03→4bb7 shared state cannot silently drift.
- suggested_fix: Add a cross-child invariant test ("det_coverage keys round-trip into the signed manifest"); optionally lift the coverage payload into the `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` so all three children share one declared shape.

═══ FINDING 2 (mode: RESIDUAL REFERENCES) — child header/What say "(P1-P7)" but the child ships P8, and P7's "escalates T4" resolves only to a suggested_fix STRING, not a wired escalation — both leave stale references a sibling/audit can mis-read ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child `012e` TITLE "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P7)..." + the "## What" enumeration ("P1...P7"); P8 lives only in the later "(Added) P8" section. Separately, "## What": "P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates T4)" and `det_floor.py:p7_destructive` (suggested_fix mentions T4); the actual T4 overlay is owned/fired by sibling `b711` (LLM-routed), with `plan_review_T4.md` shipped there.
- evidence:
  - P8 enumeration: the parent treats P8 as integral DET ("P1–P8" in the parent's T8-DET-execution-path AC + the registry "P8 reviewability/context-budget"), and `ca03`'s added AC dispatches "the P1–P8 floor". The child DOES implement P8 (`p8_reviewability`, BLOCKS) — so P8 is fully covered — but the child's primary identity (title + What) still says "P1-P7". A coverage/decomposition audit keying on the header would mis-read P8 as unowned. This is a residual stale reference, not a gap. (The G3 review on this pairing flagged the same header mismatch as a coverage-audit hazard.)
  - P7→T4 "escalation": `det_floor.py:p7_destructive` does NOT wire any T4 escalation — it emits an advisory finding whose `suggested_fix` text merely mentions "(T4)"; there is no orchestrator hook (grep `P7` in orchestrator.py → none) that forces the T4 overlay to fire when P7 hits. T4 is instead fired independently by `b711`'s overlay router. So "escalates T4" is a narrative claim that maps to a string, not a mechanism. T4 is genuinely COVERED (by b711, `plan_review_T4.md` ships) — so again NOT an absence — but the "escalates" wording is a residual reference implying a cross-child mechanism that doesn't exist.
- scenarios: (a) a registry-coverage audit keyed on the child title files P8 as an uncovered parent criterion or duplicates it into ca03; (b) an implementer reads "P7 escalates T4" and expects the DET floor to trigger the T4 overlay, when in fact T4's firing is owned wholly by b711's router independent of P7.
- impact: Low — both referents are actually implemented/owned (P8 in this child; T4 in b711); purely header/enumeration + narrative-wording residue that can mislead the next audit or implementer.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle to "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P8)..." and fold P8 into the main "## What" list so the child's primary identity matches the parent's "P1–P8" framing; AND reword "P7 ... (escalates T4)" to its true behavior ("P7 surfaces an advisory destructive finding; the T4 compat/destructiveness overlay is fired independently by b711's router — P7 does not mechanically escalate it") so no sibling/implementer expects a P7→T4 wiring that isn't there.
- suggested_fix: Title → "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P8) + fail-open polyglot tool wrappers"; change the P7 What-line to "P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (advisory; the related T4 overlay is owned/fired by `b711`, not escalated from P7)".

═══ AFFIRMED-CLEAN modes (no finding) ═══
- CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — CLEAN. 012e AC#3 "DET floor is the only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1; per-criterion LLM blocking is OPT-IN" is CONSISTENT with `487d` (Pass-3: `blocking_enabled=False` default, per-criterion `block_threshold` 0.95 opt-in) and with the parent design summary ("Defaults ship advisory; the DET floor blocks structurally"). 012e's only blocking checks (P1, P5-cycle, P8) align with the parent's "minimal sound DET floor is the only hard blocker". No contradiction.
- SCOPE OVERLAP — CLEAN (disambiguated). 012e's P8 (static pre-check: content / largest parent+child pairing exceeds the largest window → BLOCK) and ca03's runtime size-handling ladder (batch → one-criterion/call → escalate-model → too-big FAILURE FINDING) both reason about "too big", but the parent + both children EXPLICITLY disambiguate them (P8 = the static budget pre-check; ca03 = the dynamic context-window ladder), and ca03's verify notes "P8 remains the static pre-run budget check; this adds the dynamic fallback". No double-ownership collision. (Also: the container P8 parent+child-pairing clause is load-bearing for G3/G4 themselves and correctly lives in the DET owner.)
- DEPENDENCY GAP / ORDERING — CLEAN for the shipped slice. 012e consumes the 8f6c grounding oracle (prereq epic, CLOSED) per the parent prerequisite-foundations section; ca03 dispatches `run_det_floor` via the exec=DET code executor (T8 path, verify-PASS); 4bb7 signs the coverage 012e emits. The 012e→ca03→4bb7 chain has no declared `depends_on` edges (012e `deps: []`), but all three are closed + implemented + individually verify-PASS, so there is no runtime ordering gap. (The implicit-coupling RISK from the missing contract is captured as Finding 1, not as an ordering violation.)
- RESIDUAL REFERENCES (P2/P3/P4 → oracle) — CLEAN. 012e's "consume the 8f6c facade, do NOT re-build" is consistent with the parent's "Layer-1 CONSUMES this oracle — it no longer adds solidlsp/ast-grep/syft/scc/lizard as new integrations"; the stale "LSP via solidlsp" was already corrected to ctags (T1) in 012e's realignment comment, with solidlsp deferred to T2/`850f`. No residual contradiction.

NET: no G4 contradiction / scope-collision / ordering-gap that breaks the slice. Two LOW residual-reference / implicit-shared-state consistency items (DET-coverage payload contract; "(P1-P7)" title + "escalates T4" wording) — both referents are actually implemented/owned by a named child, so neither is an absence; they are drift/mis-read hazards to tidy, not coverage gaps.
